title: Uber cuts 10% of customer-service jobs and, for the first time, blames AI
excerpt: Uber has trimmed its customer-service ranks and pointed at AI. The company cut 10% of jobs in its community operations team on Wednesday. It is part of a wider push to “simplify operations, strengthen in-person collaboration, and continue to embrace AI,” a spokesperson told Bloomberg by email. It is a first for Uber. The company […]
content_html:
Uber has trimmed its customer-service ranks and pointed at AI. The company cut 10% of jobs in its community operations team on Wednesday. It is part of a wider push to “simplify operations, strengthen in-person collaboration, and continue to embrace AI,” a spokesperson told Bloomberg by email.

It is a first for Uber. The company has cut staff before. But this is the first time it has tied layoffs directly to an AI efficiency drive, Bloomberg reported. It is also the second round of cuts in under two months.

“Our organisation has become too complex and siloed,” Megha Yethadka, Uber’s vice-president of global community operations, wrote in an internal memo. Bloomberg obtained it. The team “has made some strides” with AI, she said. “But to unlock this potential, we need an effective organisation to layer AI on. We cannot scale frontier technology on top of fragmented processes.”

A familiar refrain

Uber joins a growing list. In recent months, firms from payments company Block to Oracle have named AI as a reason for job cuts. Monday.com trimmed a fifth of its staff under an “AI growth” banner. Even big banks have made the same case.

The pattern is not universal. Some leaders argue AI is changing jobs, not destroying them. Others warn that “AI efficiency” can be a tidy label for ordinary cost-cutting. Uber does not disclose the size of its customer-service arm, so the human total stays unclear.

Back to the office, too

The cuts came with a second demand. Remote workers on the team must relocate to a hub office, in line with Uber’s return-to-office rule. The reductions follow a June cut of 23% of Uber’s “people” division, which the company said was under 1% of its 34,000 staff.

Slower hiring, but not everywhere

Uber flagged the shift in May, when it said AI would slow its hiring. Even so, it is still recruiting for more than 500 roles. Some are engineers to support its robotaxi partnerships. Shares barely moved after hours.

The message under the memo is plain. Uber wants a leaner team it can wire AI into, not the sprawling one it has. Whether that lifts service or just trims payroll is the test the next few quarters will set.

Published July 23, 2026 - 12:51 pm UTC

Back to top

sentences.en: Published July 23, 2026 - 12:51 pm UTC
sentences.zh: 发布时间 UTC 时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 中午 12:51

content_html_zh:
Uber 已经削减了其客户服务队伍，并把矛头指向了人工智能。 该公司周三削减了社区运营团队 10% 的职位。 一位发言人通过电子邮件告诉彭博社，这是更广泛推动“简化运营、加强面对面协作并继续拥抱人工智能”的一部分。

这对于 Uber 来说还是第一次。 该公司此前曾裁员。 但据彭博社报道，这是该公司首次将裁员与人工智能效率提升直接挂钩。 这也是两个月内的第二轮削减。

“我们的组织变得过于复杂和孤立，”Uber 全球社区运营副总裁梅加·耶塔德卡 (Megha Yethadka) 在一份内部备忘录中写道。 彭博社得到了它。 她说，该团队在人工智能方面“取得了一些进展”。 “但为了释放这一潜力，我们需要一个有效的组织来分层人工智能。 我们无法在分散的流程之上扩展前沿技术。”

熟悉的副歌

Uber 加入了越来越多的名单。 近几个月来，从支付公司 Block 到甲骨文等公司都将人工智能列为裁员的原因。 Monday.com 在“人工智能增长”的旗帜下裁减了五分之一的员工。 就连大银行也提出了同样的理由。

这种模式并不具有普遍性。 一些领导人认为人工智能正在改变工作，而不是摧毁它们。 其他人警告说，“人工智能效率”可以成为普通成本削减的简洁标签。 优步没有透露其客户服务部门的规模，因此员工总数仍不清楚。

也回到办公室

此次削减伴随着第二项要求。 根据 Uber 的返回办公室规则，团队中的远程工作人员必须搬迁到中心办公室。 此次裁员是在 6 月份裁减 Uber“人员”部门 23% 后进行的，该公司表示，该部门在其 34,000 名员工中所占比例不到 1%。

招聘速度放缓，但并非所有地方都如此

Uber 在 5 月份就宣布了这一转变，当时它表示人工智能将放缓招聘速度。 即便如此，该公司仍在招聘 500 多个职位。 其中一些是支持机器人出租车合作伙伴关系的工程师。 盘后股价几乎没有变动。

备忘录下的信息很简单。 Uber 希望组建一支能够将人工智能融入其中的精简团队，而不是现有的庞大团队。 无论是提升服务还是只是削减工资，都是未来几个季度将面临的考验。

发布时间 UTC 时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 中午 12:51

Back to top
